message:
fix(vdbe): replace silent parse_record failures with explicit errors

The VDBE engine was calling parse_record().unwrap_or_default() in 9
locations across seek, index comparison, and conflict detection paths.
When a record was malformed, this silently produced an empty field
vector, causing incorrect comparisons, missed index entries, and
phantom conflict detection results — all without any error signal.

Replace each unwrap_or_default() with .ok_or_else() returning a
FrankenError::internal with a descriptive message identifying the
exact operation and context:
- delete_secondary_index_entries: malformed table record
- SeekGT: malformed seek key / cursor record (2 sites)
- SeekLE: malformed seek key / cursor record (2 sites)
- IdxLE/IdxGT/IdxLT/IdxGE: decode_record already returns Result
- IdxCmp: malformed index record at cursor position
- find_conflicting_rowid_in_index: malformed new key / entry record

This converts silent data corruption into visible errors that
propagate correctly through the Result chain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
